analysis.overall_assessment: The single detected tea leaf shows severe necrotic damage with extensive black/brown lesions, indicating critical health deterioration. No healthy tissue remains for standard tea production.

pollution_reduction_solutions: Apply kaolin-based foliar sprays immediately to create a protective barrier reducing ozone absorption
Install windbreaks with ozone-absorbing species (e.g., poplar, willow) around plantation perimeters
Implement real-time air quality monitoring with automated alerts for ozone spikes during high-risk seasons

immediate_actions: Halt harvesting in affected zones until pollution source is mitigated
Conduct full-field inspection to identify extent of ozone damage
Test soil and leaf samples for secondary contaminants
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: Complete rejection of all leaves showing necrotic symptoms; no healthy portions to retain
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Not suitable for orthodox tea processing; consider experimental extraction for polyphenol concentrates if economically viable
quality_preservation: Store healthy leaves from unaffected areas in nitrogen-flushed containers
Reduce withering time by 30% for any salvageable leaves to minimize oxidation

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: No usable portions for commercial tea production
alternative_uses: Experimental use in medicinal herbal blends (with safety testing)
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Chop leaves into 2cm pieces, layer with 3:1 ratio of carbon-rich materials (straw/sawdust), maintain 55-60°C for 14 days to neutralize potential phytotoxins
prevention_measures: Plant ozone-tolerant tea cultivars (e.g., 'S3A/3', 'TV1') in high-risk zones
Establish 50m buffer zones with pollution-scavenging vegetation
Schedule harvesting during early morning when ozone levels are lowest

cost_saving_opportunities: Convert rejected leaves into certified organic compost for revenue
Apply for agricultural disaster relief funds for pollution-affected crops
Reduce labor costs by temporarily shifting workers to maintenance tasks
value_recovery_methods: Partner with biotech firms for polyphenol extraction trials
Develop educational materials about pollution impacts for industry training

priority_actions: Deploy ozone monitoring system within 72 hours
Apply emergency kaolin spray to all remaining healthy plants
Initiate soil remediation with biochar application in affected zones
recommendations.timeline: Immediate (0-72h) for monitoring/spraying; 2-4 weeks for buffer zone establishment; 3-6 months for cultivar replacement
monitoring_points: New leaf emergence quality for 3 growth cycles
